zh.lifeSummary: 當Sibilla de Houghton於大約1308年出生在英國爾莫利頓時，她的父親Richard de Houghton31歲，母親Lady Sybil de Lea26歲。她於大約1330年與Robert Clitheroe在英國英格蘭藍卡爾波爾德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1388年在她的家鄉享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sibilla de Houghton于大约1308年出生在英国爾莫利頓时，她的父亲Richard de Houghton31岁，母亲Lady Sybil de Lea26岁。她于大约1330年与Robert Clitheroe在英国英格兰藍卡爾波爾德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1388年在她的家乡享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Sibilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sibella
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sibilla de Houghton was born about 1308, in Mollington, Cheshire, England, her father, Richard de Houghton, was 31 and her mother, Lady Sybil de Lea, was 26. She married Robert Clitheroe about 1330, in Bold, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1388, in her hometown, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sibilla de Houghton est née environ 1308, à Mollington, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Houghton, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Sybil de Lea, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Clitheroe environ 1330, à Bold, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1388, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sibilla de Houghton um 1308 in Mollington, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Houghton 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Sybil de Lea 26. Sie hat um 1330 in Bold, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Robert Clitheroe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1388 im Alter von 81 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sibilla de Houghton è nata il circa 1308 nel Mollington, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Richard de Houghton, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Sybil de Lea, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Robert Clitheroe circa 1330, nel Bold, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1388, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sibilla de Houghtonが約1308年にイギリスチェシュアイアモリントンで生まれた時，父Richard de Houghtonは31歳，母Lady Sybil de Leaは26歳であった。約1330年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアボルドでRobert Clitheroeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1388年に故郷で81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sibilla de Houghton(이)가 대략 1308년 영국 체실르 모링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Houghton(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Sybil de Lea(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1330년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 볼드에서 Robert Clitheroe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1388년 그녀의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sibilla de Houghton nasceu aproximadamente 1308, em Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Houghton, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Sybil de Lea, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Clitheroe aproximadamente 1330, em Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1388, em sua cidade natal, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sibilla de Houghton родилась около 1308 г. в местности моллингтон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Houghton было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Sybil de Lea, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Clitheroe около 1330 г. в местности болд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1388 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sibilla de Houghton nació aproximadamente 1308, en Mollington, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Houghton, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Sybil de Lea, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Robert Clitheroe aproximadamente 1330, en Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1388, en su pueblo, a la edad de 81 años.
